Executive technical evaluation report
Experimentation
Platforms
& Feature Flagging

Comparative analysis of GrowthBook, Optibase, PostHog, and Hotjar based on hands-on testing, including a direct BigQuery connection and A/B experiments with events in a controlled environment.

Tests performed
BigQuery · A/B testing with events
Tools evaluated
GrowthBook · Optibase · PostHog · Hotjar
Date
March 2026
Objective
Technical and functional comparison
01Positioning
What does each tool do?
GB
GrowthBookWarehouse-Native
Feature Flagging & A/B Testing
Open Source · Warehouse-Native · CUPED · Sequential Testing
Conectores: BigQuery · Snowflake · Redshift · PostgreSQL
Experimentation platform that runs on your own data warehouse. It does not move or store events — all statistics are computed from existing sources. Full control and auditability.
OB
OptibaseWebflow-Native
Native A/B testing for Webflow
No-Code · Webflow-Native · Heatmaps · Session Recording
Native Webflow Designer integration · automatic GA4
A/B testing platform built specifically for Webflow sites. Create and manage experiments directly in the Webflow Designer without leaving the environment or writing code. Includes heatmaps, session recordings, and personalization.
PH
PostHogAlternative
All-in-one product suite
Open Source · Session Replay · Heatmaps · Real-time
SDKs: JS · Python · React · iOS · Android · Go · Ruby
All-in-one suite: analytics, feature flags, A/B testing, and session replay in one system. Pricing is based on event volume, not seat count.
HJ
HotjarCRO · Behavior
Behavior Analytics & CRO
Heatmaps · Session Replay · Surveys · A/B Testing
Snippet JS · Integraciones: GA4 · Segment · HubSpot · Slack
Behavior analytics platform focused on heatmaps, session recordings, and surveys. Includes A/B testing and personalization. Built to explain why users behave as they do, not only what they do.
02Proof of Concept
Tool evaluation

GrowthBook and PostHog were evaluated with direct tests in our own environment (BigQuery + real events). Optibase and Hotjar do not fit that technical context — their assessment relies on official documentation, verified reviews on G2, Capterra, and Gartner, and documented cases from real users.

GrowthBookDirect PoC
Test A — BigQuery connection
Confirmed
BigQuery integration completed successfully
Native connection without moving data. Warehouse governance stays intact — data never leaves your own infrastructure.
Confirmed
SQL queries visible and auditable by the team
Every statistical calculation exposes the SQL executed. The team can replicate any result from BigQuery.
Consider
Initial setup requires a technical profile
Credentials, fact tables, and SQL metrics must be defined. It is not plug-and-play for non-technical roles.
Test B — A/B testing with events
Confirmed
A/B testing run with real BigQuery events
Consistent variant assignment. CUPED and sequential testing available from the first experiment.
Consider
Results are not real-time
They rely on on-demand warehouse queries. No continuous streaming.
100%
Data in own warehouse
SQL
Auditable by the team
CUPED
Variance reduction
BigQuery
Integration confirmed
PostHogDirect PoC
Test — A/B testing with events
Confirmed
Full setup in minutes with no prior infrastructure
A/B experiment configured and started in minutes. No prior warehouse. Everything happens inside the same platform.
Confirmed
Results updated in real time
The dashboard reflects experiment status continuously. Easier to spot anomalies.
Confirmed — key differentiator
Session replay filtered by experiment variant
Session recordings filtered by variant. Connects quantitative results with real user behavior.
Consider
More limited advanced statistics
Granular CUPED and advanced sequential testing are less configurable than in GrowthBook.
Consider
Data lives on PostHog infrastructure
In cloud mode, events are stored outside your own infrastructure. Self-hosting is available.
Real-time
Continuous results
One
Single system for the full flow
Replay
Session by variant
Externo
Data outside warehouse
OptibaseExternal evaluation
Sources: G2, Capterra, Product Hunt, official documentation
Documented
Native Webflow setup: first tests in minutes
G2 and Capterra users confirm the install (Webflow Designer app + API key) completes smoothly. No developer required once the script is live.
Documented
Tests on CTAs, headlines, and full layouts
Documented Webflow agency cases running A/B tests on copy, button colors, and full pages. Results are easy to read without advanced statistics knowledge.
Documented
Heatmaps and recordings integrated with tests
Behavior data (clicks, scroll, sessions) correlates directly with experiment variants in the dashboard.
Consider
Webflow-only — not applicable to other stacks
Does not work outside the Webflow ecosystem. No integration with BigQuery, Snowflake, or external warehouses.
Consider
Basic statistics — no CUPED or sequential testing
Uses Probability to Be Best (P2BB) as the main metric. Suitable for web CRO but not advanced statistical experimentation.
4.7/5
Rating G2 (verificado)
Webflow
Only supported stack
P2BB
Statistical metric
~30 min
Tiempo a primer test
HotjarExternal evaluation
Sources: Gartner Peer Insights, Capterra, verified reviews 2025–2026
Documented
5-minute install via GTM or direct snippet
Capterra and Gartner reviewers agree: heatmaps and recordings live within 24 hours with no extra setup. Non-technical teams operational in under a day.
Documented
Heatmaps and session recordings as core strength
Consistently cited as the go-to tool for observing user behavior. Click, scroll, and move maps available on all plans.
Documented
On-site surveys integrated with session recordings
E-commerce and SaaS teams report improved lead rates and conversion funnels by pairing heatmaps with NPS surveys.
Consider
Hotjar does not run A/B tests — it analyzes them
It does not create variants or assign traffic. It acts as a qualitative layer on tests run elsewhere (GrowthBook, Optimizely, etc.). A common point of confusion for new users.
Consider
More features paywalled with each update
Gartner and Capterra reviewers note the basic plan has been progressively trimmed and pricing increased after the Contentsquare acquisition.
4.3/5
Rating Gartner (verificado)
24h
Heatmaps activos tras setup
No
No ejecuta A/B tests propios
CRO
Qualitative analysis layer
02bVisual evidence
Screenshots from the tests performed
GrowthBook — Experiment INTENTO2
Experiment results — Results tab
Metric: CTA click · Engine: Bayesian
Variant A: 50% (5/10 users) · Variant B: 35.3% (6/17 users). The experiment shows "Not enough data" for Chance to Win — higher traffic volume is needed to reach statistical significance.
Health check — traffic balance across variants
No Sample Ratio Mismatch · p-value = 0.178
Variant A: 10 real users (37%) vs 13.5 expected (50%). Variant B: 17 users (63%) vs 13.5 expected. Acceptable distribution — GrowthBook detected no anomalies in experiment balance.
BigQuery — event table connected to GrowthBook
439 events recorded · Dataset: analytics_events · Table: events
Events experiment_viewed and cta_click flowing in real time from the application to BigQuery. Columns: event_name, event_time, page_url, experiment_id (home_test), variation_id. GrowthBook queries this table directly to compute experiment results.
PostHog — Experiment home-test-ab
Experiment metrics — exposures and primary metrics
Exposures: 5 total · Control 2 (40%) · Test 3 (60%)
Experiment started on 03/20/2026. The exposures chart shows continuous growth for both variants. The exposure criterion is $feature_flag_called.
Metric: home views (funnel) · "Not enough data yet"
Control: 100% (2/2) · Test: 100% (3/3). Both variants convert at 100% but the sample is too small. PostHog shows "Not enough data yet" — consistent with the experiment sample size.
Microsoft Clarity — Observing traffic for the GrowthBook experiment
Context: Microsoft Clarity was set up as an observability layer on the same Webflow site (ab-test-lab.webflow.io) where the GrowthBook + BigQuery experiment runs. It independently recorded sessions for users exposed to variants, confirming real traffic arrived and the CTA was the only element clicked.
Session recordings — filter: click on #cta_b
2 recorded sessions from ab-test-lab.webflow.io, filtered by click on #cta_b (experiment variant B). Both sessions: Colombia · Chrome · desktop. Duration: 4–5 sec. Each session logged exactly 1 click.
Heatmap — click area on the CTA
2 page views · 2 clicks · CTR 100%. The only clicked element was the CTA button. The heatmap confirms there was no interaction with other page elements during the experiment.
2
Recorded sessions
100%
CTR recorded
#cta_b
Tracked element
Webflow

06Setup
Setup ease
GrowthBook
3 / 5Requires a technical profile
Connect the warehouse, define metrics in SQL, and install the SDK. A data-engineering workflow. Extensive documentation and active Slack support.
  • 01Create a Cloud account or deploy with Docker
  • 02Connect BigQuery credentials
  • 03Define fact tables and metrics in SQL
  • 04Install the SDK and expose user attributes
  • 05Create the experiment, assign metrics, and launch
Optibase
5 / 5Native Webflow setup, no code
Direct Webflow Designer integration. First live A/B test in under 30 minutes. No developer required after the script is installed.
  • 01Create an account on optibase.io (free plan available)
  • 02Install the app from the Webflow marketplace
  • 03Connect the project with an API key from the dashboard
  • 04Configure variants and conversion events in the Designer
  • 05Activate the experiment and monitor results
PostHog
4 / 5High ease of use, live in minutes
A JavaScript snippet and events start capturing immediately. A reverse proxy is optional but recommended in production.
  • 01Create an account — no credit card
  • 02Insert the JS snippet in the app
  • 03Define conversion events as metrics
  • 04Create an A/B experiment and configure variants
  • 05Activate and monitor in real time
Hotjar
5 / 5Simplest on the market
One JS snippet and heatmaps and recordings start immediately. A/B testing (Business plan) needs extra setup but remains accessible to non-technical roles.
  • 01Create an account — free Basic plan, no card
  • 02Insert the JS snippet on the site or enable an integration
  • 03Heatmaps and recordings turn on automatically
  • 04Configure surveys or A/B tests from the dashboard
07Metrics & Analytics
Metric analysis capabilities
GrowthBook
Statistical model
Bayesian and frequentist. The team chooses the approach. Results as probability of improvement or p-values and significance.
CUPED
Reduces time to significance using pre-experiment covariates. Useful with small samples. Pro plan.
Sequential Testing
Continuous monitoring without inflating false positives. Allows early stopping with statistical validity. Pro plan.
SQL metrics
Defined on warehouse tables. Proportion, mean, ratio, percentile. Reusable fact tables across experiments.
Multi-Arm Bandits
Dynamic traffic allocation toward the best-performing variant. Pro plan.
Transparencia
Every result exposes the SQL executed. Fully auditable by the data team.
Optibase
Native A/B testing
Experiments configured directly in the Webflow Designer. Code-free variants, results in the native dashboard.
Custom conversions
Tracks clicks, form submits, scroll, and any site interaction. No manual code configuration.
Heatmaps
Per-variant heatmaps to show attention vs ignored areas. Complements quantitative test data.
User recordings
Real sessions recorded and filtered by experiment variant. Connects metrics to observed behavior.
GA4 integration
Automatic sync with Google Analytics 4 when GA4 is present. Test events and conversions visible in GA4.
PostHog
Product Analytics
Funnels, retention, paths, trends, and correlations. No SQL required — all visual.
Real-time experiments
Any captured event can be a metric. Results update continuously as events arrive.
Session replay + experiment
Recordings filtered by variant. Connects quantitative results with real user behavior.
Dashboards and alerts
Team-shareable dashboards with automatic alerts on configured metrics.
LLM Analytics (2026)
Language-model tracking: latency, cost per call, tokens consumed, error rate.
Hotjar
Heatmaps
Click, move, and scroll heatmaps per page. Surfaces high-attention zones vs ignored elements. Core feature on all plans.
Session Recordings
Full session recordings with filters by device, source, and behavior. Rage clicks and JS errors flagged automatically.
Surveys
On-site and post-session surveys with conditional logic. NPS, CSAT, and open questions tied to observed behavior.
Behavior funnels
Step-by-step visualization to conversion with per-stage drop-off. Plus plan and above.
A/B Testing (Business+)
Visual, no-code tests from the editor. Basic conversion outcomes. No advanced statistics such as CUPED.

09Use cases
When does each tool fit?
GrowthBook
Where it fits well...
  • Your own warehouse (BigQuery, Snowflake, Redshift) as the data source
  • Full control — data never leaves your own infrastructure
  • Advanced statistics: CUPED, sequential testing, bandits
  • Full auditability of every experiment calculation in SQL
  • Predictable per-user cost without depending on event volume
  • Open-source platform you can fork and extend
Optibase
Where it fits well...
  • The site is built on Webflow and you need native, no-code A/B testing
  • The team is marketing or design — no dedicated technical roles
  • You need fast testing: first live test in under 30 minutes
  • You want session recording and heatmaps integrated with experiments
  • Limited budget ($0–$289/mo by volume) without requiring a warehouse
PostHog
Where it fits well...
  • You need session replay tied directly to experiment variants
  • A large team where per-seat cost is a budget constraint
  • No existing warehouse and you want to start immediately with your own events
  • Full product analytics in one system is the priority
  • You need support across web, iOS, and Android
Hotjar
Where it fits well...
  • The focus is understanding user behavior more than statistical conversion measurement
  • Heatmaps, session recordings, and surveys are the primary toolkit
  • CRO or UX wants to observe the site without warehouse-sourced data
  • Pairs with another tool (GrowthBook or PostHog) for the statistical layer
  • Tight budget: the free plan is useful for low-traffic sites
Evaluation — March 2026 · Pricing subject to change
growthbook.io optibase.io posthog.com hotjar.com
